chunk_summary: Guidelines for pediatric acid suppression in chronic cardiorespiratory disease emphasize indication-driven use, reassessment plans, and pediatric stop rules to minimize risks like infections and exacerbations.
learning_objectives: Understand the risks associated with chronic PPI use in pediatric populations with cardiorespiratory disease.
Learn criteria for prescribing PPIs, including GERD complications and therapeutic trials.
Apply pediatric stop rules for PPI therapy, including reassessment and deprescribing protocols.
key_facts: PPI exposure in young children is associated with higher risk of serious infections (Lassalle et al., 2023).
Chronic PPI therapy in cystic fibrosis correlates with increased pulmonary exacerbations and hospitalizations (Ayoub et al., 2017).
Reassess PPI therapy after 4–8 weeks; taper and stop if symptoms improve.
PPIs should not be prescribed for weight gain or slow feeding without clear indications.
Document indication, start date, and stop date in the nutrition plan to ensure deprescribing during follow-up.
reference_quote: PPI exposure in young children is associated with higher risk of serious infections (Lassalle et al., 2023).
document_key_topics: Risks of chronic PPI use in cardiorespiratory disease
Pediatric stop rules for PPI therapy
